payload.content: Built configurable policy profile layer for AIegis. Added PyYAML dependency, src/aiegis/policy_profiles.py, tests/test_policy_profiles.py, examples/policies.yaml, CLI flags --policy-file and --policy-profile for inspect-html and inspect-email, README docs, plan update. Loader validates YAML strictly: named profiles under profiles, only approval_required_actions and blocked_actions_on_prompt_injection keys, action lists must contain strings. Verification before commit: pytest --cov=aiegis --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=90 => 29 passed, total coverage 91.86%; ruff check . => all checks passed; mypy src => success no issues in 8 source files. Committed f6d2cfa feat: add configurable policy profiles. git status clean on main.

payload.summary: Added deterministic tool-call policy firewall to AIegis. New tool_firewall module defines ToolCallRequest, ToolCallPolicy, ToolCallDecision, and evaluate_tool_call. Rules block explicitly blocked tools and secret-bearing external calls, require approval for sensitive tools/external targets, and allow low-risk local calls. MCP now exposes aiegis.evaluate_tool_call with structuredContent results. Verification passed: pytest --cov=aiegis --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=90 (50 passed, 91.27%), ruff check ., mypy src.

payload.summary: Added standalone local JSONL audit sink alongside optional Eventloom. JsonlAuditSink appends schema_version/event_type/timestamp/policy_profile/payload JSONL records, supports content audit records and tool-call decisions, creates parent directories, and has deterministic clock injection for tests. CLI now supports --audit-log for inspect-html/inspect-email and mcp-stdio. MCP appends JSONL audits for content inspection and aiegis.evaluate_tool_call decisions when configured. README and plan updated. Verification passed: pytest --cov=aiegis --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=90 (59 passed, 91.79%), ruff check ., mypy src.

payload.summary: Hardened local JSONL audit sink to minimize and redact by default. Content audit JSONL payloads now omit raw text, quarantined segment bodies, and raw links, replacing them with finding metadata, counts, source/trust labels, severity, and decision. Tool-call audit payloads redact sensitive argument values by key for token, secret, password, credential, authorization, and api_key-like names. README and plan updated. Verification passed: pytest --cov=aiegis --cov-report=term-missing --cov-fail-under=90 (61 passed, 91.77%), ruff check ., mypy src.
